title: Question: Allow inlining of interfaces
body:
---


Author Name: **Johan Bjork**
Original Redmine Message: 1798 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi,

Our projects use a lot of parameterized interfaces, which results in hundreds of un-inlined interface modules (tiny, only containing variables!) cells in the final design. I haven't profiled it yet, but I would suspect that it prevents quite a few optimizations both in Verilator and also once the c++ compiler gets it's hands on it.

Wilson, any ideas on how to best add support for this? In V3Inline you comment that
```
// Inlining an interface means we no longer have a cell handle to resolve to.
// If inlining moves post-scope this can perhaps be relaxed.

```
Can you elaborate a bit more on this idea?

Thanks
/Johan
code_blocks:
// Inlining an interface means we no longer have a cell handle to resolve to.
// If inlining moves post-scope this can perhaps be relaxed.
